title: Compound Plating
author: Opentrons
description: This protocol performs a custom compound plating protocol as specified by a user-input .csv file. You can download an example .csv template [here](https://opentrons-protocol-library-website.s3.amazonaws.com/custom-README-images/056543/ex.csv)! The same tip is used for each source, and the user is prompted to refill the deck state if all destination plates have been processed.
steps_raw: This protocol performs a custom compound plating protocol as specified by a user-input .csv file. You can download an example .csv template [here](https://opentrons-protocol-library-website.s3.amazonaws.com/custom-README-images/056543/ex.csv)! The same tip is used for each source, and the user is prompted to refill the deck state if all destination plates have been processed.
Download your protocol and unzip if needed.
Upload your custom labware to the OT App by navigating to `More` > `Custom Labware` > `Add Labware`, and selecting your labware files (.json extensions) if needed.
Upload your protocol file (.py extension) to the OT App in the `Protocol` tab.
Set up your deck according to the deck map.
Calibrate your labware, tiprack and pipette using the OT App. For calibration tips, check out our support articles.
materials_raw: Opentrons 24 Tube Rack with Eppendorf 1.5 mL Safe-Lock Snapcap
Opentrons P20 Single Channel Electronic Pipette (GEN2)
